{{Quote|Some ask, ''are we still ourselves?'' Are we truly Jedi? We honor those questions, we still seek the correct path. But, as always, we pay a price to do so. Today the price was Master Cei Vookto.|Mace Windu's eulogy for Cei Vookto, the Jedi General who perished in the battle|<ref name="Jedi: Mace Windu">''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]''</ref>}}
The '''Battle of Lianna''' was a battle fought on [[Lianna/Legends|Lianna]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Victory on Lianna [[Death/Legends|cost]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Cei Vookto]] [[Gender/Legends|his]] life.

==Background==
When the call came for [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] and Masters to take command of elements of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], Master Vookto returned immediately from his mission in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] to become a [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]]. He led his [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] into combat on Lianna, to protect a [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] starfighter facility from invading [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] forces. With [[Raith Sienar/Legends|Raith Sienar's]] designers working on new starfighters for the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]], it was not a planet the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] could afford to lose.

==The battle==
The battle raged across Lianna, with losses on both sides. Leading the campaign, Master Vookto used his powers of elemental summoning to cleanse the planet with twin pillars of water and fire. The toll this took on Cei Vookto cost him his life.

==Aftermath==
With victory assured, and the Sienar facility safe, a funeral was held for the fallen Master Vookto. He was cremated in a funeral temple on Lianna, with [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]], [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]], his former padawan, [[Dama Montalvo]], and several clone troopers in attendance. With his death, the remnants of his forces were handed over to [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]].

After the funeral, [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] received a report from [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] and [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] regarding dissident Jedi who had refused to participate in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The leaders of the dissidents had been united by [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]] and had gathered on [[Ruul (moon)|Ruul]] at Bulq's deteriorating family estate. They agreed to a meeting, but requested the presence of one of the Council members who could speak for the entire body, specifically Mace Windu. Windu decided to go.

During the presentation of the report, Windu and Quinlan Vos engaged in a sparring match. However, towards the end of the mock duel, Windu advised Vos not to hold back. This spurred Vos to unleash a vicious maneuver that Windu recognized as a [[Form VII/Legends|Vaapad technique]], prompting him to bring a quick end to the sparring session. When questioned about it, Vos claimed it was a maneuver taught to him by Sora Bulq during his retraining, although Bulq said nothing about Vaapad.

Before Windu left for Ruul, Yoda advised him to look at Bulq with a 'child's eye', free from preconception. Windu agreed to meditate on this.
